macro_segments.title: The Debate Between Rabbi Yochanan and Reish Lakish on a Father's Power to Make His Son a Nazir

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Tannaitic Debate: The Age Limit for a Father's Imposition of Nazirut
Rabbi's Position: Nazirut by Father Based on Halacha (Torah Law) Until Age 13 with Two Pubic Hairs
Rabbi Yose b'Rabbi Yehuda's Position: Father's Power Based on Chinuch (Training) Until Age of Making Vows (12)
Gemara's Reconciliation: Whether the Debate is About Tannaitic Sources or Two Different Theories
First Approach: Both Agree Nazirut is a Halacha, but Disagree on Status of Mufla Samuch L'Ish (Near-Adult Vow-Maker)
Discussion of Halacha L'Moshe MiSinai and Its Meaning in the Context of Nazirut Parameters
Second Approach: Both Agree It's Based on Chinuch, Disagreeing on Whether Son's Vow Independence Overrides Father's Chinuch Role

macro_segments.title: The Story of Rabbi Chanina and the Question of When Adulthood Terminates Paternal Imposition of Nazirut

micro_segments.title: The Baraita: Rabbi Chanina's Father Made Him a Nazir, and Rabban Gamliel Tests His Adult Status
Rabbi Chanina's Response: Either Way I'm a Nazir—Whether I Was Minor or Adult
Rabban Gamliel's Praise: Recognition That This Young Man Will Become a Halachic Sage
Gemara's Question: What If He Became Adult in the Middle of the 30-Day Period?
Resolution: Mufla Samuch L'Ish Status Does Not Interrupt, But Full Adulthood Does
Chanina's Clever Solution: Accepting Nazirut Upon Himself Regardless of When He Became Adult

macro_segments.title: The Son's Right to Use the Father's Dedicated Funds for His Own Nazir Sacrifices

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: A Son Can Use Father's Dedicated Funds, But a Daughter Cannot
Case One: Son Becomes Nazir After Father Dies, Stipulating Use of Father's Funds
Case Two: Son and Father Are Both Nazirs Simultaneously—Debate on Fund Accessibility
Rabbi Yochanan: This Is a Halacha (Tradition)—No Source, Not Based on Inheritance Alone
Why Only Sons and Not Daughters: Even Daughters Who Would Inherit Cannot Use These Funds
Baraita: Clarifying Rabbi Yosi's Position on When Son Can Access Father's Funds

macro_segments.title: Multiple Sons Sharing Father's Dedicated Nazir Funds and Inheritance Laws

micro_segments.title: Rava's Question: Do Two Sons Split the Father's Funds, or Does First-Come-First-Served Apply?
Whether This Halacha Follows Inheritance Laws (Yerusha) or Is Independent
Rava's Second Question: Does the Firstborn (Bechor) Get a Double Portion from Nazir Funds?
Rosh's Interpretation: Distinguishing Between Chulin (Ordinary) and Hekdesh (Consecrated) Property
Why Bechor Rules May Not Apply to Hekdesh Funds: The Concept of 'All That Is Found'
Whether the Halacha Applies When Father and Son Are Different Types of Nazirs (Olam vs. Stam)
Rav Ashi's Question: If Father Was Tamei and Son Is Tahor, Can Son Use Father's Tamei-Related Funds?
